question_text: A car's speed-time graph shows it accelerating from 0 m/s to 20 m/s over 8 seconds, then maintaining constant speed for 12 seconds. Which statement best evaluates the car's motion?

options.label: A
options.text: The car travels the same distance in both sections because the time spent accelerating is less than the constant speed section

options.label: B
options.text: The car travels further during constant speed because the area under that section of the graph is larger

options.label: C
options.text: The acceleration is 20 m/s² throughout the first section

options.label: D
options.text: Both sections show the same average speed of 10 m/s

correct_answer: B) The car travels further during constant speed because the area under that section of the graph is larger
explanation: In a speed-time graph, area under the line equals distance. First section (triangle): 0.5 × 8 × 20 = 80 m. Second section (rectangle): 20 × 12 = 240 m. The constant speed section has a larger area, so the car travels further. The acceleration is 20 ÷ 8 = 2.5 m/s², not 20 m/s².
